message: I've never been the 'prepared one' in my friend group—I'm usually the person asking to borrow a phone charger at 11 PM. But last spring when that severe weather system rolled through, I actually had my bases covered: fresh water, a battery radio, flashlights that worked (because I'd checked them), even a list of everyone's phone numbers written down. And you know what? It felt *good*. Not smug-good, but genuinely calm. My roommate was frantically searching for candles while I was making coffee on the camping stove like it was totally normal. For once, I wasn't scrambling. This little refresher on the basics really helped me see that being ready doesn't mean you have to be a doomsday person—it just means you get to skip the panic part. Have you ever had a moment where being prepped actually paid off in the most satisfying way?
